rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks using valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign statements
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when referring to ports
Use visible identifiers and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions; use exposed ports or constants instead
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations to avoid resolution errors
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for contract expressions
Initialize temporal recurrence with a base value, e.g., 0.0 -> pre(t) + step
Use nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE expressions
Declare constants with valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool)
anti_patterns: Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names like integer or boolean
Avoid type mismatches between helper declarations and expressions
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
